The National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS) has declared the NEET SS 2026 result on January 23, 2026. This exam is for admission to DM, MCh, and DrNB super-specialty courses.

**Key Dates:**
*   Exam Date: December 26-27, 2025
*   Result Declaration: January 23, 2026
*   Scorecard Release: February 2-3, 2026
*   Round 1 Counseling: March 10-25, 2026
*   Round 2 Counseling: March 28-April 16, 2026

1.  Visit [natboard.edu.in](natboard.edu.in) or [nbe.edu.in](nbe.edu.in).

*   Rank
*   Percentile
*   Qualification Status

**Cutoff Marks (Examples):**
*   Medical Group: 225
*   Paediatric Group: 271
*   Anaesthesiology Group: 284

**Important Note:** The minimum qualifying percentile is the 50th percentile for all categories. Qualified candidates should participate in MCC counseling.

website_content.title: CBSE Class 12 Economics Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## CBSE Class 12 Economics Answer Key 2026

The CBSE Class 12 Economics Board Exam 2025–26 will be conducted on **March 18, 2026**, from **10:30 AM to 1:30 PM** (3 hours).

The paper is worth **80 marks** (theory) + **20 marks** (internal/project work) = **100 marks** total.

**Key Details:**

*   **Conducting Board:** Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
*   **Exam Name:** CBSE Class 12 Economics 2026
*   **Academic Session:** 2025–26
*   **Exam Date:** March 18, 2026
*   **Exam Time:** 10:30 AM – 1:30 PM
*   **Total Marks:** 100 (80 Theory + 20 Project/Internal Assessment)
*   **Unofficial Answer Key:** Available after 1:30 PM on March 18, 2026
*   **Official Answer Key:** As per CBSE/subject experts (if released)

**Exam Pattern:**

*   **Part A:** Introductory Macroeconomics (40 marks)
*   **Part B:** Indian Economic Development (40 marks)

**Question-Wise Marks Distribution:**

*   MCQs: 20 questions (1 mark each) - 20 marks
*   Short Answer Type I: 4 questions (3 marks each) - 12 marks
*   Short Answer Type II: 6 questions (4 marks each) - 24 marks
*   Long Answer Questions: 4 questions (6 marks each) - 24 marks

**How to Calculate Your Score:**

1.  1 mark for each correct MCQ (no negative marking).
2.  Allot marks proportionately for descriptive questions based on expert solutions.
3.  Add theory marks (out of 80) + 20 marks for internal/project work.

**Download Unofficial Answer Key:**

1.  Visit a trusted education portal.
2.  Search for “CBSE Class 12 Economics Answer Key 2026”.
3.  Select your question paper set.
4.  Download the PDF and match your answers.

website_content.title: AP Inter 2nd Year Chemistry Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## AP Inter 2nd Year Chemistry Answer Key 2026 (Unofficial)

The AP Inter 2nd Year Chemistry exam for the 2025–26 academic year was held on March 18, 2026, from 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM. The exam is worth 100 marks (60 theory + 40 practicals).

**Key Details:**

*   **Conducting Board:** Board of Intermediate Education, Andhra Pradesh (BIEAP)
*   **Exam Name:** AP Inter 2nd Year Chemistry 2026
*   **Academic Year:** 2025–26
*   **Exam Date:** March 18, 2026
*   **Exam Time:** 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM
*   **Mode:** Offline (Pen-and-paper)
*   **Total Marks:** 100 (60 Theory + 40 Practicals)
*   **Unofficial Answer Key Availability:** After 12:00 PM on March 18, 2026

**Exam Analysis:**

*   **Section A (Very Short Answer/Objective):** Easy, 2 marks per question, 1-10 questions, Total 20 marks
*   **Section B (Short Answer):** Easy to Moderate, 4 marks per question, 11-16 questions, Total 24 marks
*   **Section C (Long/Descriptive):** Moderate, 8 marks per question, 17 & 18 questions, Total 16 marks

**Passing Criteria:** Students must secure at least 35% marks in each subject, including theory and practical components.

**Disclaimer:** The unofficial answer key is for reference only. Final scores will be based on BIEAP’s official evaluation.

website_content.title: ICSE Class 10 Geography Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## ICSE Class 10 Geography Answer Key 2026 (Unofficial)

The Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) conducted the ICSE Class 10 Geography (H.C.G. Paper 2) exam on **March 18, 2026**, from **11:00 AM to 1:00 PM**.

An unofficial answer key is prepared by subject experts shortly after the exam. Students can use it to cross-check answers for Part I & II, understand difficulty, and estimate their score (out of 80).

### Exam Highlights

| Particulars | Details |
|---|---| 
| Conducting Board | Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) |
| Exam Name | ICSE Class 10 Geography (H.C.G. Paper 2) 2026 |
| Exam Date | March 18, 2026 (Wednesday) |
| Exam Time | 11:00 AM - 1:00 PM |
| Reading Time | 10:45 AM - 11:00 AM (15 minutes) |
| Duration | 2 hours |
| Total Marks | 100 (80 Theory + 20 Internal Assessment) |
| Unofficial Answer Key | Available after 1:00 PM on March 18, 2026 |

### Paper Pattern & Marks Distribution

**Part I (Compulsory) - 30 Marks**
*   Question 1: Interpretation of a Topographical Map (10 Marks)
*   Question 2: Map of India – pointing and labelling (10 Marks)
*   Question 3: Objective / Short Answer / MCQs (10 Marks)

**Part II - 50 Marks**
*   Any 5 out of 9 structured questions (10 marks each)

**Total (Theory) - 80 Marks**

*Internal Assessment: 20 Marks (Project/Practical)*

**Answer Key Link:** [Click Here](https://example.com/icse-geo-answer-key-2026) *(This is a placeholder link)*

### Exam Analysis

*   **Part I:** Easy to Moderate (Topographical Map, Map of India, Objective/Short Answer)
*   **Part II:** Moderate (Structured/Long Answer Questions)

Detailed analysis will be updated after the exam concludes.

website_content.title: WBJEE 2026 Exam Schedule
website_content.markdown_content:
## WBJEE 2026 Exam Schedule

The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board (WBJEEB) has released the exam schedule for WBJEE 2026 for admissions to B.Tech, B.Pharm, and B.Arch programs in West Bengal.

**Key Dates:**

*   **Notification Release:** March 13, 2026
*   **Registration:** February - April 2026
*   **Admit Cards:** April - May 2026
*   **Exam Date:** May 24, 2026
*   **Results:** June 2026
*   **Counselling:** July - August 2026

**Important Dates:**

*   **Application Start:** Early February 2026
*   **Last Date to Apply:** April 5, 2026
*   **Provisional Answer Key:** Late May 2026
*   **Final Results:** Early June 2026
*   **Counselling Begins:** Mid-July 2026

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   Passed Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics with at least 45% aggregate (40% for reserved categories).
*   Minimum age of 17 years as of December 31, 2026.

**Preparation Tips:**

*   Focus on NCERT Class 11-12 syllabus for Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.
*   Practice with past papers, emphasizing speed for the OMR format.

**Exam Pattern:**

*   Paper 1 (Maths): 100 marks, 2 hours
*   Paper 2 (Physics + Chemistry): 100 marks, 2 hours

*   Application Window Open: 16 March 2026
*   Application Window Close: 06 April 2026

**Apply Mode:** Online

website_content.markdown_content:
## MHT CET 2026 Exam – Quick Overview

**Exam Name:** Maharashtra Common Entrance Test (MHT CET)
**Conducting Authority:** State Common Entrance Test Cell, Maharashtra (CET Cell Maharashtra)
**Exam Year:** 2026
**Exam City Slip Status:** Released (via Admit Card)

**Exam Dates:**
* Session 1: PCM – April 11 to 19, 2026
* Session 1: PCB – April 21 to 26, 2026
* Session 2: PCB – May 10 to 11, 2026
* Session 2: PCM – May 14 to 17, 2026

**Mode of Exam:** Computer-based test (online)

**Key Information:**

*   The exam city information will be available on the **admit card**, not a separate slip.
*   Admit cards are expected to be released in early April 2026 for Session 1 (3-4 days before the exam).
*   Download from: [cetcell.mahacet.org](cetcell.mahacet.org) → MHT CET 2026 section → Admit Card / Hall Ticket link.

**Admit Card Details:**

*   Candidate Name
*   Application Number / Roll Number
*   Allotted Exam City
*   Exam Date and Group (PCM / PCB)
*   Shift Timing
*   Exam Centre Name and Address
*   Candidate’s Photograph and Signature
*   Important Exam-Day Instructions

**Important Reminders:**

*   Plan travel and accommodation early.
*   Review exam-day instructions.
*   Keep admit card and ID proof ready.
*   Stay updated on [cetcell.mahacet.org](cetcell.mahacet.org).

website_content.title: DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026 - Apply Now!

The Diploma Certificate Entrance Competitive Examination (DCECE) is conducted by the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB) for admission to polytechnic diploma courses in Bihar.

**Important Dates:**

| Item | Details (2026) |
|---|---| 
| Application Start Date | 19 March 2026 |
| Last Date to Apply | 21 April 2026 |
| Admit Card Release Date | May 2026 |
| Correction Window | As per official notice on BCECEB website |
| Exam Date | To be announced |

**Eligibility Criteria:**

| Stream | Qualification | Subjects | Domicile | 
|---|---|---|---| 
| Diploma Engineering (PE) | Passed Class 10th with 35% marks | Science, Mathematics, English | May apply |
| Para Medical (PM) & Pharmacy (PMM) | Passed Class 12th with Science (PCB) | Physics, Chemistry, Biology | May apply |

**Documents Required:**

*   Active mobile number and email ID
*   Aadhaar card or valid ID proof
*   Class 10th & 12th mark sheets (photocopies & scanned copies)
*   Domicile certificate (if applicable)
*   Passport-size photograph (< 100 KB)
*   Scanned signature

**Application Process:**

1.  Visit the official BCECEB website.
2.  Register for a new account.
3.  Login and fill the application form.
4.  Upload photo and signature.
5.  Verify and preview the form.
6.  Pay the application fee (General/OBC: ₹1000 approx., SC/ST: ₹500 approx.).
7.  Download and print the application form.

**Common Mistakes to Avoid:**

*   Incorrect category/caste details
*   Name mismatch
*   Blurry/oversized photos
*   Payment issues
*   Missing the correction window

website_content.markdown_content:
## NIMCET 2026 Syllabus – Overview

The NIT MCA Common Entrance Test (NIMCET) is a national-level entrance exam for admission to Master of Computer Applications (MCA) programs in various National Institutes of Technology (NITs) and select IIITs.

**Detail Information:**

*   **Exam Name:** NIT MCA Common Entrance Test (NIMCET)
*   **Conducting Body:** National Institutes of Technology (NITs) – Rotating (NIT Tiruchirappalli for 2026)
*   **Course:** MCA Admission
*   **Mode:** Online (Computer-Based Test)
*   **Duration:** 2 Hours
*   **Total Questions:** 120
*   **Total Marks:** 1000

**NIMCET Exam Pattern 2026:**

| Subject                          | Number of Questions | Marks per Correct Answer | Negative Marking per Wrong Answer | Total Marks |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------- | ----------- |
| Mathematics                      | 50                  | 12                       | 3                                 | 600         |
| Analytical Ability & Logical Reasoning | 40                  | 6                        | 1.5                               | 240         |
| Computer Awareness               | 20                  | 6                        | 1.5                               | 120         |
| General English                  | 10                  | 4                        | 1                                 | 40          |
| **Total**                       | **120**             | **--**                   | **--**                            | **1000**      |

*Negative marking: 25% of the marks for that question (applied section-wise).
*No penalty for unanswered questions.

**Key Syllabus Areas:**

*   **Mathematics:** Algebra, Calculus, Coordinate Geometry, Probability, Trigonometry, Set Theory
*   **Analytical Ability & Logical Reasoning:** Verbal & Non-verbal Reasoning, Data Interpretation
*   **Computer Awareness:** Computer Basics, Data Representation, Hardware & Software, Internet & Email
*   **General English:** Reading Comprehension, Vocabulary, Grammar

website_content.title: MPPSC SSE 2026 Notification
website_content.markdown_content:
# MPPSC Syllabus 2026

The Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC) State Service Exam (SSE) 2026 is a significant opportunity for candidates seeking government jobs in Madhya Pradesh. This exam recruits for positions like Deputy Collector, Naib Tehsildar, and others.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission State Service Exam (MPPSC SSE)
*   **Conducting Body:** Madhya Pradesh Public Service Commission (MPPSC)
*   **Stages:** Prelims, Mains, Interview
*   **Mode:** Offline (Prelims - Objective, Mains - Descriptive)

**Exam Pattern:**

**MPPSC Prelims Exam Pattern:**

| Paper | Subject | Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|---| 
| Paper 1 | General Studies | 100 | 300 | 2 Hours |
| Paper 2 | General Aptitude Test (CSAT) | 100 | 300 | 2 Hours |
| **Total** |  |  | **600** |  |

*Paper 1 marks are considered for final merit.*

*Negative Marking: 1/3 mark deducted for each wrong answer.*

**MPPSC Mains Exam Pattern:**

| Paper | Subject | Duration | Marks |
|---|---|---|---| 
| General Studies I | History, Culture, Geography | 3 Hours | 300 |
| General Studies II | Polity, Governance, Social Issues | 3 Hours | 300 |
| General Studies III | Economy, Science, Environment, Technology | 3 Hours | 300 |
| General Studies IV | Ethics, Integrity, Aptitude | 3 Hours | 200–300 |
| General Hindi | Hindi Language | 3 Hours | 200 |
| Hindi Essay | Essay Writing | 2 Hours | 100 |

**Syllabus Highlights:**

*   **Prelims:** General Science, Current Events, History of India & MP, Indian Polity & Economy, Geography.
*   **Mains:** Detailed syllabus for each General Studies paper and Hindi.

**Important Dates & Resources:**

Visit the official MPPSC website for the latest updates, notification, application form, and syllabus PDF.

website_content.markdown_content:
## MPESB ITI Training Officer Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (MPESB) has released the answer key for the ITI Training Officer examination held on **February 27, 2026**. Candidates can download the provisional answer key from the official website.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Madhya Pradesh Employees Selection Board (MPESB)
*   **Post Name:** ITI Training Officer
*   **Total Vacancies:** 1120
*   **Exam Date:** February 27, 2026
*   **Answer Key Released:** March 18, 2026
*   **Last Date for Objection:** March 21, 2026
*   **Objection Fee:** ₹XX per question (refundable if accepted)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [esb.mp.gov.in](esb.mp.gov.in)
2.  Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section.
3.  Click on "MPESB ITI Training Officer Answer Key 2026".
4.  Log in with your Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth.
5.  Download the PDF.

**How to Raise Objection:**

1.  Log in to your candidate portal on [esb.mp.gov.in](esb.mp.gov.in).
2.  Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section.
3.  Select the question(s) to challenge.
4.  Upload supporting documents (if required).
5.  Pay the objection fee.
6.  Submit before March 21, 2026.

**Important Dates:**

*   Exam Conducted: February 27, 2026
*   Answer Key Released: March 18, 2026
*   Objection Deadline: March 21, 2026

website_content.title: Kerala Plus Two Economics Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Kerala Plus Two Economics Answer Key 2026

The Directorate of Higher Secondary Education (DHSE) Kerala conducted the Plus Two Economics exam on **March 19, 2026**, from **9:30 AM to 12:15 PM** (including a 15-minute cool-off time).

The exam is for **100 marks** (80 marks theory + 20 marks internal assessment).

**Unofficial Answer Key:** Available after 12:15 PM on March 19, 2026.

**Official Answer Key:** To be released by DHSE Kerala (Tentative - March/April 2026).

### Exam Highlights:

*   **Conducting Board:** Directorate of Higher Secondary Education, Kerala (DHSE)
*   **Exam Name:** Kerala Plus Two Economics 2026
*   **Exam Date:** March 19, 2026
*   **Exam Time:** 9:30 AM - 12:15 PM
*   **Total Marks:** 100 (80 Theory + 20 Internal Assessment)
*   **Mode of Exam:** Offline (Pen and paper)

### Section-wise Marks Distribution:

*   **Section A:** Objective/Multiple Choice, Very Short (1 & 2 Marks) - Easy
*   **Section B:** Short Answer (3 Marks) - Easy to Moderate
*   **Section C:** Short Essay (4 Marks) - Moderate
*   **Section D:** Long Essay (5 & 8 Marks) - Moderate to Difficult

**Note:** A minimum of 30% is required separately in the Terminal Evaluation (TE) and overall to qualify.

website_content.markdown_content:
# APPSC Group 2 Syllabus 2026

## Overview

The APPSC Group 2 Services Exam is a competitive recruitment process conducted by the Andhra Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC) for various executive and non-executive posts in Andhra Pradesh state government departments.

**Exam Name:** APPSC Group 2 Services Exam
**Conducting Body:** Andhra Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC)
**Stages:** Prelims (Screening Test), Mains, Computer Proficiency Test (CPT)
**Mode:** Offline
**Question Type:** Objective (MCQs)

## Exam Pattern

### APPSC Group 2 Prelims Exam Pattern

| Subject                     | Questions | Marks | Duration | 
|-----------------------------|-----------|-------|----------|
| General Studies & Mental Ability | 150       | 150   | 150 Minutes |
| **Total**                   | 150       | 150   | 150 Minutes |

### APPSC Group 2 Mains Exam Pattern

| Paper | Subject / Topics                               | Questions | Marks | Duration |
|-------|------------------------------------------------|-----------|-------|----------|
| Paper 1 | Social History of Andhra Pradesh & Indian Constitution | 150       | 150   | 150 Minutes |
| Paper 2 | Indian & AP Economy & Science & Technology       | 150       | 150   | 150 Minutes |
| **Total** |                                                | 300       | 300   | - |

**Note:** Objective MCQs. Negative marking of ⅓ mark for wrong answers. Level: Degree standard.

## Prelims Syllabus

*   **Indian History (30 marks):** Ancient, Medieval, Modern
*   **Geography (30 marks):** Physical, Economic, Human (India & Andhra Pradesh focus)
*   **Indian Society (30 marks):** Structure, Issues, Welfare
*   **Current Affairs (30 marks):** National, International, Andhra Pradesh-specific
*   **Mental Ability:** Logical reasoning, Series/coding-decoding, Basic numeracy, Data analysis

## Mains Syllabus

### Paper 1 – Social History of Andhra Pradesh & Indian Constitution

*   **Social & Cultural History of Andhra Pradesh (75 marks):** Pre-historic dynasties, Medieval kingdoms, Europeans, Socio-cultural awakening, Nationalist movements, Andhra Movement, Formation of Andhra State.
*   **Indian Constitution (75 marks):** Salient features, Preamble, Fundamental Rights/Duties, Federal/Unitary aspects, Legislature/Executive/Judiciary, Centre-State relations, Constitutional bodies, Commissions, Electoral reforms, Decentralization.

### Paper 2 – Indian & AP Economy & Science & Technology

*   **Indian & AP Economy (75 marks):** National income/planning/reforms, Money/banking, Sectors, AP-specific.
*   **Science & Technology (75 marks):** S&T policy, space, defence, ICT, nuclear/renewable energy, Environment, Health.

## Preparation Tips

*   Prioritize Andhra Pradesh-specific topics.
*   Read standard books + official Bare Acts/Constitution.
*   Practice previous year papers and mocks.
*   Stay updated with current affairs.
*   Focus on data interpretation and reasoning.

website_content.title: APPSC CDPO Syllabus 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
# APPSC CDPO Syllabus 2026

This syllabus is for the Andhra Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC) CDPO exam in 2026.

**Exam Pattern:**

| Section | Subject | Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|---| 
| Section 1 | General Studies & Mental Ability | 150 | 150 | 150 Minutes |
| Section 2 | Home Science / Social Work / Sociology | 150 | 150 | 150 Minutes |

**Section 1: General Studies & Mental Ability**
*   Current Affairs (National & International)
*   Indian Constitution & Polity
*   Indian Economy & Planning
*   Geography (India & World, with focus on Andhra Pradesh)
*   History of India & Andhra Pradesh
*   Environmental Issues & Disaster Management
*   Science & Technology Developments
*   Logical Reasoning
*   Analytical Ability
*   Data Interpretation
*   Number Series & Sequences
*   Coding-Decoding
*   Problem Solving

**Section 2: Home Science / Social Work / Sociology**
*   Child Development (Growth & Development, ECCE, Psychology)
*   Nutrition (Balanced Diet, Malnutrition, Food Science)
*   Family Resource Management & Health & Hygiene
*   Community Development & Social Welfare Programs (ICDS)
*   Government Schemes for Women & Children
*   Women Empowerment & Family Welfare
*   Social Work (Concepts, Methods, Poverty, Welfare Programs)

**Exam Details:**
*   Objective Type (Multiple Choice Questions)
*   Each question carries 1 mark
*   Total Marks: 300

website_content.title: RBI Grade B Officer Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RBI Grade B Officer Exam 2026

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) conducts the Grade B Officer exam for DR – General posts. This highly competitive recruitment process assesses banking, economic, financial, and analytical skills.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Conducting Body:** Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
*   **Stages:** Phase 1, Phase 2, Interview
*   **Mode:** Online
*   **Question Type:** Objective (Phase 1) + Objective & Descriptive (Phase 2)

**Phase 1 Exam Pattern:**

| Subject             | Questions | Marks | Duration | |
| ------------------- | --------- | ----- | -------- | |
| General Awareness   | 80        | 80    | 25 mins  | |
| Quantitative Aptitude | 30        | 30    | 25 mins  | |
| English Language     | 30        | 30    | 25 mins  | |
| Reasoning           | 60        | 60    | 45 mins  | |
| **Total**           | **200**   | **200**| **120 mins**| |

*Negative marking of ¼ mark (0.25) for each wrong answer. Sectional timing applies.*

**Phase 2 Exam Pattern:**

*   **Paper 1: Economic & Social Issues (ESI)** - 100 marks, 120 mins (30 mins objective + 90 mins descriptive)
*   **Paper 2: English (Writing Skills)** - 100 marks, 90 mins (Descriptive)
*   **Paper 3: Finance & Management (FM)** - 100 marks, 120 mins (30 mins objective + 90 mins descriptive)

**Syllabus Highlights:**

*   **General Awareness:** Current Affairs, Banking & Financial Awareness, RBI functions, Indian Financial System.
*   **Quantitative Aptitude:** Simplification, Profit & Loss, Data Interpretation.
*   **Reasoning:** Puzzles, Syllogism, Coding-Decoding.
*   **English Language:** Reading Comprehension, Error Spotting, Vocabulary.
*   **ESI:** National Income, Indian Economy, Globalization, Social Structure in India.
*   **Finance & Management:** Financial System, Financial Markets, Risk Management, Management Fundamentals.

**Interview Process:**

*   75 marks (Personality Test assessing banking knowledge, current affairs, communication skills).
*   Final merit based on Phase 2 + Interview marks.

**Apply Online:** Visit the official RBI recruitment portal when the notification is released.

**Other Active RBI Recruitment 2026**

*   Bank Medical Consultant Jobs - Last Date: 03-04-2026
*   Medical Consultant Jobs - Last Date: 27-03-2026

website_content.title: NCERT Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## NCERT Admit Card 2026 OUT! 

The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has released the admit card for the Non-Teaching recruitment (Group A, B & C posts) on **19 March 2026**. 

The Computer Based Test (CBT) is scheduled for **24, 25 & 27 March 2026**. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Release Date:** 19 March 2026
*   **Exam Dates:** 24, 25 & 27 March 2026
*   **Total Vacancies:** 173 Posts
*   **Advertisement No.:** 01/2025/Non-Academic
*   **Exam Mode:** Online (CBT)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website: ncert.nic.in
2.  Navigate to the "Latest Announcements" or "Vacancies/Recruitment" section.
3.  Click on the "NCERT Admit Card 2026 for Group A, B & C Non-Teaching Posts" link.
4.  Enter your Registration Number/Application Number, Date of Birth/Password, and Captcha Code.
5.  Download and save the PDF.

**Important Documents to Carry:**

*   NCERT Admit Card 2026 (printed copy)
*   Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport)
*   Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies)

**Pro Tip:** Check all details on your admit card immediately after download and report any discrepancies within 24 hours.

website_content.title: West Bengal Judicial Service Admit Card 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
**West Bengal Judicial Service Admit Card 2026 OUT!**

Public Service Commission, West Bengal (WBPSC) has released the admit card for the Judicial Service (Civil Judge - Junior Division) Preliminary Examination.

**Key Details:**

*   **Release Date:** 16 March 2026
*   **Exam Dates:** 23 March to 2 April 2026 (23rd, 24th, 25th, 28th, 29th, 30th March, and 1st & 2nd April)
*   **Official Website:** psc.wb.gov.in

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit psc.wb.gov.in
2.  Navigate to the "Admit Card / Download Call Letter" or "Latest Announcements" section.
3.  Click on "West Bengal Judicial Service Admit Card 2026".
4.  Enter your Registration Number/Enrollment No. and Date of Birth/Password.
5.  Download and print the admit card (2-3 copies recommended).

**Important Documents to Carry:**

*   Admit Card (printed copy)
*   Valid Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport)
*   Passport-size photographs (2 copies)
*   Blue/Black ballpoint pen

**Pro Tip:** Check all details on your admit card immediately after download and report any discrepancies to WBPSC within 24 hours.

website_content.markdown_content:
## SSC CHSL Tier 1 Final Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the final answer key for the SSC CHSL Tier 1 examination held from **November 12th to 30th, 2025**. Candidates can now download their answer keys, response sheets, and scorecards.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
*   **Exam Name:** Combined Higher Secondary Level (CHSL) Tier 1
*   **Exam Date:** November 12th - 30th, 2025
*   **Answer Key Status:** Released (Final)
*   **Release Date:** March 19th, 2026
*   **Last Date to Download:** April 9th, 2026 (Tentative)
*   **Total Vacancies:** Approx. 3131 (for CHSL 2025 cycle)

**Important Links:**

*   [Download Final Answer Key & Response Sheet](https://ssc.gov.in)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website: [ssc.gov.in](https://ssc.gov.in)
2.  Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Candidate's Corner" section.
3.  Click on the relevant link for CHSL Tier 1.
4.  Log in with your Registration Number/ID and Password.
5.  Download your answer key and scorecard.

**Cut-Off Trends (Expected):**

| Category | Expected Cut Off (Out of 200) |
|---|---| 
| General (UR) | 140 - 155 |
| OBC | 130 - 145 |
| SC | 120 - 135 |
| ST | 110 - 125 |
| EWS | 130 - 145 |
| PwD | 90 - 110 |

**Scoring:**
* Correct Answer: +2 marks
* Wrong Answer: -0.50 marks
* Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.markdown_content:
## IIFCL Assistant Manager (Grade A) 2026 Recruitment

**Organization:** India Infrastructure Finance Company Limited (IIFCL)
**Post Name:** Assistant Manager (Grade A)
**Exam Stages:** Test I (Online), Test II (Online), Interview
**Mode of Exam:** Online (Computer-Based)
**Question Type:** Objective (MCQs)
**Total Questions / Marks:** 150 / 200
**Duration:** 120 Minutes (60 min per Test)
**Negative Marking:** 0.25 marks deducted for each wrong answer

**Test I Pattern (100 Questions / 100 Marks, 60 Minutes):**
*   Reasoning: 25 Questions / 25 Marks
*   Quantitative Aptitude: 25 Questions / 25 Marks
*   English Language: 25 Questions / 25 Marks
*   Current Affairs (Finance Sector): 25 Questions / 25 Marks

**Test II Pattern (50 Questions / 100 Marks, 60 Minutes):**
*   Domain Knowledge / Professional Knowledge: 50 Questions / 100 Marks

**Syllabus Highlights:**
*   **Test I:** Reasoning (Puzzles, Syllogism, etc.), Quantitative Aptitude, English, Finance Sector Current Affairs.
*   **Test II:** Project Finance, Corporate Banking, Risk Management, Indian Economy, Financial & Banking System in India.

**Selection Process:** Online Test + Interview

website_content.title: Odisha CGL 2026 Syllabus & Exam Pattern
website_content.markdown_content:
## Odisha CGL Syllabus 2026 (OSSC Combined Graduate Level Recruitment Examination)

The OSSC CGL recruitment exam follows a two-stage process: Prelims (qualifying) and Mains (merit-based).

**Exam Pattern:**

**Prelims:**
*   Arithmetic: 25 Questions
*   Data Interpretation: 25 Questions
*   Logical Reasoning: 25 Questions
*   Current Events: 25 Questions
*   Computer Awareness: 25 Questions
*   Odisha GK: 25 Questions
*   Negative Marking: 0.25

**Mains:**
*   **Paper 1: Language (Odia & English)** - 100 Marks, 2.5 Hours
*   **Paper 2: General Studies** - 100 Marks, 2.5 Hours
*   No descriptive paper in Mains.

**Syllabus Details:**

**Prelims Syllabus:**
*   Arithmetic & Data Interpretation: Number System, Fractions, Percentage, Ratio & Proportion, Data Interpretation (Charts, Graphs).
*   Logical Reasoning: Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning, Analogies, Problem-Solving.
*   Current Events: National & International Affairs, History, Polity, Economy, Geography, Science, Sports.
*   Computer Awareness: Fundamentals, MS Office, Internet, Operating Systems.
*   Odisha GK: Geography, Economy, History, Culture.

**Mains Syllabus:**
*   **Paper 1 (Language):** English (Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary), Odia (Grammar, Essay Writing, Letter Writing).
*   **Paper 2 (General Studies):** Indian Constitution, Indian Economy, Odisha Economy, History of India & Odisha, Geography, General Science, Art & Culture.

**Selection Process:**
1.  Preliminary Examination
2.  Mains Written Examination
3.  Certificate Verification
4.  Skill Test / Computer Proficiency Test (for specific posts)

**Download Syllabus PDF:**
Visit [https://www.ossc.gov.in](https://www.ossc.gov.in) and navigate to the Recruitment/Notifications section to find the CGLRE-2025/2026 advertisement and download the detailed syllabus PDF.

website_content.title: APPSC Endowment Officer Syllabus 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## APPSC Endowment Officer Syllabus 2026

The APPSC Endowment Officer Syllabus 2026 (for Executive Officer Grade-III in A.P. Endowments Sub-Service) is based on previous recruitment patterns (2021-2025). The syllabus focuses on General Studies & Mental Ability and Hindu Philosophy & Temple System.

**Exam Pattern:**

**Preliminary Examination (Qualifying)**
| Section | Subject | Questions | Marks |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | General Studies & Mental Ability | 50 | 50 |
| B | Hindu Philosophy & Temple System | 100 | 100 |
| **Total** |  | **150** | **150** |

*Negative marking of 1/3 for wrong answers.*

**Mains Examination (Written Test)**
| Paper | Subject | Questions | Marks | Duration |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Paper 1 | General Studies & Mental Ability | 150 | 150 | 150 Minutes |
| Paper 2 | Hindu Philosophy & Temple System | 150 | 150 | 150 Minutes |

*Negative marking of 1/3 for wrong answers. Final merit based on Mains performance.*

**Syllabus Highlights:**

*   **General Studies & Mental Ability:** Current Affairs, History of India & Andhra Pradesh, Polity, Economy, Science, Logical Reasoning, Data Interpretation, Disaster Management, Bifurcation of Andhra Pradesh.
*   **Hindu Philosophy & Temple System:** Ramayanam, Mahabharatham, Bhagavatham, Puranas, Temple Agamas, Hindu Festivals, Vedic Culture, Philosophies & Gurus, Temple Income & Duties of Executive Officers, Endowments Act.

**Selection Process:**
1.  Preliminary Exam (Qualifying)
2.  Mains Examination (Merit-based)
3.  Document Verification

**Preparation Tips:**
*   Prioritize AP-specific topics.
*   Revise current affairs daily.
*   Practice previous year papers and mock tests.
*   Strengthen logical reasoning and data interpretation.
*   Focus on conceptual clarity in temple administration and Hindu philosophy.

Osmania University has announced the results for the Telangana State Eligibility Test (TG-SET-2025) on **March 20, 2026**.  

The exam was conducted from **December 22nd to 24th, 2025**. Candidates can now download their scorecards and check their qualifying status.

**Key Details:**

*   **Conducting Body:** Osmania University on behalf of Telangana State
*   **Result Date:** March 20, 2026
*   **Exam Dates:** December 22nd, 23rd, and 24th, 2025
*   **Purpose:** Eligibility for Assistant Professor / Lecturer positions in Telangana Universities & Colleges

**How to Check:**

1.  Visit [telanganaset.org](http://telanganaset.org).

**Qualifying Criteria:**

*   Top 6% of candidates qualify overall.
*   Minimum aggregate: 40% for General/EWS, 35% for reserved categories.

**Exam Dates:** 29 March to 01 April 2026
**Download Status:** Active Now
**Advertisement No.:** A-1/E-1/2025

1. Visit the official website: uppsc.up.nic.in
2. Navigate to the "Download Admit Card" section.

*   Recent passport-size photographs (if specified)
*   Black/Blue ballpoint pen

**Exam Day Instructions:**
*   Reach the exam center 45-60 minutes early.
*   No entry after gate closure.
*   Follow all invigilator instructions.

website_content.markdown_content:
## SSB Paramedical Recruitment 2026

The Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), Ministry of Home Affairs, Government of India, is seeking applications for **76 posts** of Head Constable (Laboratory Assistant), Head Constable (Physiotherapy Assistant), and Constable (Nursing Assistant cum Medic).

**Important Dates:**
* Advertisement Publication: 21 March 2026
* Last Date to Apply: Approximately 20 April 2026 (30 days from 21 March 2026)
* Admit Card/PET-PST Date: To be notified
* Written Examination Date: To be notified

**Posts & Pay Scale:**
* Head Constable (Laboratory Assistant): 30 posts, Level-4 (₹25,500–₹81,100)
* Head Constable (Physiotherapy Assistant): 12 posts, Level-4 (₹25,500–₹81,100)
* Constable (Nursing Assistant cum Medic): 34 posts, Level-3 (₹21,700–₹69,100)

**Eligibility Criteria:**
* **Age:** 18-27 years (Relaxation applicable for SC/ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, and others - see details in the full notification)
* **Education:** Varies by post (see full notification for details)
* **Experience:** Minimum one year of relevant experience.

**Selection Process:**
1. Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
2. Physical Standard Test (PST)
3. Written Examination (Computer Based Test)
4. Documentation & Medical Examination

**Application Fee:**
* UR, EWS, OBC: ₹100 (Non-Refundable)
* SC, ST, Ex-Servicemen, Female Candidates: Exempted

**Apply Online:** [https://recruitment.ssb.gov.in/](https://recruitment.ssb.gov.in/) or [https://ssb.gov.in/](https://ssb.gov.in/)

**Download Notification:** [Link to Notification PDF - not provided in text]

website_content.title: Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Odisha B.Ed Entrance Exam 2026 Notification

The Higher Education Department, Government of Odisha, has released the notification for admissions to B.Ed, B.H.Ed, and M.Ed courses for the academic session 2026–27.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** Odisha B.Ed Entrance Examination 2026
*   **Conducting Body:** Higher Education Department, Government of Odisha through SAMS Odisha
*   **Courses Offered:** B.Ed, B.H.Ed, M.Ed
*   **Application Mode:** Online via SAMS Odisha portal

**Important Dates:**

*   Short Notification: 20 March 2026
*   Online CAF Start Date: 23 March 2026 (11:00 AM)
*   Last Date to Apply: 06 April 2026 (11:45 PM)
*   CAF Editing Window: 07 April 2026 (11:00 AM – 11:45 PM)
*   Admit Card Availability: 27 May 2026 (02:00 PM onwards)
*   Exam Date: 31 May 2026 (Sunday)

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   Bachelor's degree with 50% aggregate marks (45% for reserved categories).
*   Engineering/Technology graduates need 55% aggregate.
*   Indian citizen, ordinarily a permanent resident of Odisha.

**Application Fee:**

*   General/Unreserved/SEBC: ₹500
*   SC/ST/PwD: ₹300

**Exam Pattern:**

*   Offline, OMR-based
*   100 MCQs for 100 marks
*   Duration: 2 hours
*   Sections: General English, General Awareness, Reasoning Ability, Teaching Aptitude, and subject-specific components.

For more details, refer to the official SAMS Odisha portal.

## Delhi DDA Patwari & Junior Engineer Final Result 2026 OUT!

The Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has released the final result (provisional select list/merit list) for the following posts under Advt. No. 09/2025:

*   **Patwari** (Post Code-23)
*   **Junior Engineer (Civil)** (Post Code-15)
*   **Junior Engineer (Electrical/Mechanical)** (Post Code-16)

**Important Dates:**

*   Junior Engineer (Elect./Mech.): 10th March 2026
*   Junior Engineer (Civil) & Patwari: 20th March 2026

**Exam Dates:**
* Computer-Based Examination: 16th December 2025 - 3rd January 2026
* Skill Tests: March 2026 (for some posts)

**Selection Process:** Written Test → Skill Test (if applicable) → Document Verification → Medical Examination

**How to Check:**

1.  Visit [dda.gov.in](dda.gov.in).
2.  Navigate to the "Latest Jobs" or "Recruitment" section.

**Next Steps:** Selected candidates will be called for Document Verification and Medical Examination. Keep checking the official website for updates.

website_content.markdown_content:
## TSRTC Supervisor Trainee Recruitment 2026

The Telangana State Level Police Recruitment Board (TSLPRB) has released the hall ticket for the Traffic Supervisor Trainee (TST) and Mechanical Supervisor Trainee (MST) recruitment in Telangana State Road Transport Corporation (TSRTC).

**Important Dates:**
*   **Admit Card Release Date:** 23 March 2026
*   **Exam Date:** 29 March 2026 (Sunday)
*   **Application Last Date:** 20 January 2026

**Total Vacancies:** 198 Posts (84 TST + 114 MST)

**Exam Mode:** Offline (Objective MCQ Type)

**How to Download Hall Ticket:**
1.  Visit the official TSLPRB website: [tgprb.in](tgprb.in)
2.  Navigate to the "Supervisor Trainees Recruitment 2025 in TGSRTC" section or "Latest Announcements".
3.  Click on "Download Admit Card" or "TSLPRB TSRTC Hall Ticket 2026" link.
4.  Enter your Registration Number, Date of Birth/Password, and Verification Code.
5.  Download and save the PDF file.

**Documents Required:**
*   TSLPRB TSRTC Hall Ticket 2026 (printed copy)
*   Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Driving License, Voter ID Card, Passport)
*   2 Passport-size photographs
*   Black/Blue ballpoint pen

**Important Instructions:**
*   Reach the exam center 45-60 minutes before reporting time.
*   No entry after gate closure.
*   Follow all invigilator instructions.

**Helpdesk:** 9391005006 or support@tslprb.in

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
*   **Post Name:** Storekeeper
*   **Vacancies:** Multiple (as per Advt. No. 13 of 2025)
*   **Answer Key Status:** Released (Provisional / Objection Notice)
*   **Release Date:** 22 March 2026
*   **Objection Deadline:** Check official notice (typically 5-7 days from release)
*   **Official Website:** [sssb.punjab.gov.in](sssb.punjab.gov.in)

**How to Download & Raise Objections:**

1.  Visit the official website: [sssb.punjab.gov.in](sssb.punjab.gov.in)
2.  Navigate to the "Latest News / Notifications" or "Answer Key" section.
3.  Locate the "Objections Notice – Storekeeper (Advt. No. 13 of 2025)" dated 22 March 2026.

5.  Submit objections online (if applicable) through the candidate portal, following the instructions in the notice.
6.  Pay the objection fee (if applicable).

**Scoring Scheme (Typical):**

*   Correct Answer: +1 mark
*   Wrong Answer: -0.25 mark (negative marking)
*   Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.title: Karnataka 2nd PUC English Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Karnataka 2nd PUC English Answer Key 2026

The Karnataka 2nd PUC English Answer Key 2026 has not been officially released by the Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) as the exam took place on March 3, 2026.

**Exam Overview:**

*   **Exam Name:** Karnataka 2nd PUC English Board Examination 2026
*   **Conducting Authority:** Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB)
*   **Class:** 2nd PUC (Class 12)
*   **Exam Date:** March 3, 2026
*   **Mode:** Offline
*   **Total Marks:** 80 (Theory) + Internal Assessment
*   **Duration:** 3 Hours + 15 Minutes Reading Time
*   **Answer Key Status:** Not Released (Official expected late March 2026)

**Unofficial Answer Key (Selected Questions):**

*   **Q1:** In *Romeo and Juliet*, ______ is described as “gentle, loving and black-browed”.  Answer: b) night
*   **Q2:** In *Too Dear!*, ______ was the initial punishment to the criminal. Answer: c) death sentence
*   **Q3:** According to the speaker in *On Children*, “life does not tarry ______”. Answer: d) yesterday
*   **Q4:** Match the items in Column A with Column B with reference to *A Sunny Morning*. Answer: b) A-iii, B-i, C-ii
*   **Q5:** In *The Gardener*, ______ bedecked himself with gold, built a mansion and invited scholars to his house. Answer: c) Basavaiah

**Release Date (Expected):**

The official Karnataka 2nd PUC English Answer Key 2026 is expected to be published in the last week of March 2026.

**How to Download (When Available):**

1.  Visit the official KSEAB website: [kseab.karnataka.gov.in](kseab.karnataka.gov.in)
2.  Navigate to “Examinations” > “Answer Key” or “2nd PUC” section.
3.  Select “2nd PUC Answer Key 2026” and choose English.
4.  Download the subject-wise PDF.

**Marks Distribution (Indicative):**

*   Objective/MCQs: 10 marks
*   Short Answers: 40 marks
*   Long Answers: 6 marks
*   Comprehension: 10 marks
*   Grammar & Writing: 24 marks

## Kerala Class 11 Physics Answer Key 2026 (Unofficial)

An unofficial answer key is being prepared by subject experts. Students can use it to estimate their marks.

**Exam Highlights:**

website_content.markdown_content:
## Punjab Agricultural University (PAU) Recruitment 2026

**Overview:**

Punjab Agricultural University (PAU) has announced a recruitment notification for Teaching and Non-Teaching Posts.

**Important Dates:**

*   **Online Registration Start:** 21 March 2026
*   **Online Registration End:** 17 April 2026
*   **Last Date for Hard Copy Submission:** 27 April 2026 (up to 4:00 p.m.)

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   **Age Limit:** 18 - 37 years
*   **Qualification:** Matric-level Punjabi language certificate (mandatory), Bachelor’s/Master’s/Ph.D./NET/SLET/SET (as per post requirement).

**Application Fee:**

*   **General/OBC:** ₹1298 (most posts), ₹354 (Clerk & Bee Keeper), ₹118 (Ward Attendant)

**Post Details & Fees:**

*   Dean Postgraduate Studies: ₹1,298
*   Dean, College of Basic Sciences & Humanities: ₹1,298
*   Director, Gurdev Singh Khush Institute: ₹1,298
*   Senior Biotechnologist: ₹1,298
*   Food Technologist (Milling/Packaging): ₹1,298
*   Botanist, Molecular Cytogeneticist, Plant Biotechnologist, Entomologist, Soil Chemist, Assistant Professor (various): ₹1,298
*   Assistant Librarian: ₹1,298
*   District Extension Scientist: ₹1,298
*   Clerk: ₹354
*   Bee Keeper: ₹354
*   Ward Attendant: ₹118

**Selection Process:**

*   Shortlisting

**Important Note:** Hard copy submission is mandatory. Separate application and fee for each post.

| Exam Time | 10:30 AM – 1:30 PM |
| Reading Time | 10:15 AM – 10:30 AM |
| Exam Mode | Offline (Pen and Paper) |
| Total Marks | 100 (80 Theory + 20 Internal Assessment) |
| Unofficial Answer Key | Available after 1:30 PM on March 23, 2026 |

**Sample Questions & Answers:**

1. Which one of the following is NOT a consequence of 'Shock Therapy'?

|---|---|---|

**Marking Scheme:**

| Section | Type | Marks Distribution |
|---|---|---| 
| Section A | MCQs | 1 Mark Each |
| Section B | Short Answer | 2–4 Marks Each |
| Section C | Long Answer | 6 Marks Each |
| Section D | Case-Based Questions | 4–6 Marks Each |

*Note: Final marks will include 20 marks for internal assessment/project work.*

website_content.title: WB JENPAS UG 2026 Registration
website_content.markdown_content:
## WB JENPAS UG 2026 Registration Details

The West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board (WBJEEB) has announced the registration for JENPAS UG 2026 for admission to BSc Nursing, BPT, BMLT, and other paramedical courses.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organizing Body:** WBJEEB
*   **Courses Offered:** BSc Nursing, BPT, BMLT, etc.
*   **Registration Start Date:** March 24, 2026
*   **Last Date for Submission:** April 15, 2026
*   **Correction Window:** April 17-19, 2026
*   **Exam Date:** June 7, 2026
*   **Application Fee:** ₹500 (General), ₹400 (SC/ST/OBC) + bank charges
*   **Mode:** Online Only
*   **Eligibility:** Class 12 or equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Mathematics (50% aggregate, 40% for reserved categories). Minimum age 17 years as of December 31, 2026.

**Registration Steps:**

1.  Visit wbjeeb.nic.in/jenpas-ug and select "JENPAS UG 2026 Registration."
2.  Register with email, mobile number, and password.
3.  Log in and enter personal & academic details.
4.  Upload passport photo & signature.
5.  Choose exam papers (Paper 1 &/or Paper 2).
6.  Review, pay the fee, and submit.
7.  Download confirmation page.

**Important Dates:**

*   Registration Starts: March 24, 2026
*   Form Submission Ends: April 15, 2026
*   Form Correction: April 17-19, 2026
*   Admit Card Release: May 2026 (TBA)
*   Exam Date: June 7, 2026
*   Result Declaration: June 2026 (TBA)

website_content.title: ISC Class 12 Economics Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## ISC Class 12 Economics Answer Key 2026

The ISC Class 12 Economics theory examination for the 2025–26 academic year is scheduled for **Monday, March 23, 2026**, from **2:00 PM to 5:00 PM**, with a reading time from **1:45 PM to 2:00 PM**.

This unofficial answer key, prepared by subject experts, allows students to cross-check their answers and estimate their scores before official results are released.

### Exam Highlights

| Particulars           | Details                                  |
|-----------------------|------------------------------------------|
| Conducting Board      | Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE) |
| Exam Name             | ISC Class 12 Economics Exam 2026        |
| Class                 | Class 12                                 |
| Exam Date             | March 23, 2026                           |
| Exam Time             | 2:00 PM - 5:00 PM                        |
| Reading Time          | 1:45 PM - 2:00 PM                        |
| Exam Mode             | Offline (Pen and Paper)                  |
| Total Marks           | 80 Marks (Theory)                        |

### Sample Questions & Answers

*   **Question:** Which one of the following will be included in Capital Account of Balance Payments of India?
    **Answer:** Rush Ltd., a foreign car company, invested in India for production
*   **Question:** Demand curve for a commodity...
    **Answer:** 38
*   **Question:** ICB Bank receives a cheque of ₹25,000 from Azim Bank. ICB Bank claims it through the central bank of the country.
    **Answer:** Clearing house facility

### Marks Distribution

| Section    | Type                  | Marks Distribution |
|------------|-----------------------|--------------------|
| Section A  | Short Answer Questions | 2-4 Marks Each     |
| Section B  | Long Answer Questions  | 6-8 Marks Each     |
| Total      |                       | 80 Marks           |

**Note:** This is an unofficial answer key for reference only. Final marks will be awarded by CISCE.

## UP Co-operative Bank Special Recruitment 2026

The U.P. Co-operative Institutional Service Board, Lucknow, has announced a special recruitment drive for 116 vacancies in District Co-operative Banks and U.P. State Construction Co-operative Federation across Uttar Pradesh.

**Post Details:**

*   **Manager:** 7 posts (Category-1)
*   **Junior Manager:** 45 posts (Category-2)
*   **Assistant/Cashier (Banking Assistant/Clerical Staff):** 57 posts (Category-3)
*   **Assistant/Typist:** 2 posts (Category-3)
*   **Assistant Engineer (Civil):** 5 posts (Category-2)

**Eligibility:**

*   Exclusively for candidates belonging to Scheduled Caste and Other Backward Classes of Uttar Pradesh.
*   Candidates must meet post-wise educational qualification, age limit, and other service conditions as prescribed in the detailed notification.

**Important Dates:**

*   **Online Application Start Date:** 25 March 2026
*   **Last Date for Submission of Online Examination Fee:** As per schedule in detailed notification
*   **Online Application Last Date:** 15 April 2026

website_content.title: ICHR Junior Research Fellowship 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## ICHR Junior Research Fellowship 2026

The Indian Council of Historical Research (ICHR) has announced 80 Junior Research Fellowships (JRF) for the year 2026.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Indian Council of Historical Research (ICHR)
*   **Post Name:** Junior Research Fellowship
*   **Number of Posts:** 80 (with reservation for SC, ST & PwD)
*   **Fellowship Amount:** ₹17,600/- per month + ₹16,500/- Contingency Grant per annum
*   **Eligibility:** Ph.D. Programme in Historical Studies (Ancient/ Medieval/ Modern India) at a recognized university
*   **Online Application Start Date:** 20-03-2026
*   **Last Date for Online Submission:** 10-04-2026
*   **Last Date for Printout Submission:** 17-04-2026
*   **Application Mode:** Online + Hard Copy
*   **Application Fee:** ₹500/- (NIL for SC/ST/PWD)

**Selection Process:**

*   Stage-I: Online Entrance Test (100 Marks)
*   Stage-II: Presentation-cum-Interview (60 Marks)

**Important Note:** Submission of both online application and hard copy with unique ID is mandatory.

## Arunachal Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC) TGT Recruitment 2026

APPSC has announced a recruitment drive for **389 Trained Graduate Teacher (TGT)** posts under the Education Department, Government of Arunachal Pradesh.

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Arunachal Pradesh Public Service Commission (APPSC)
*   **Advertisement Number:** 02/2026
*   **Post Name:** Trained Graduate Teacher (TGT)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 389
*   **Pay Scale:** Pay Matrix Level-7 (₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400)
*   **Eligibility:** Second-Class Bachelor’s Degree in relevant subject + B.Ed (General)
*   **Application Mode:** Online
*   **Selection Process:** Written Exam (Objective) + Viva-Voce & Classroom Teaching

**Important Dates:**

*   **Notification Release Date:** 23rd March 2026
*   **Application Start Date:** 26th March 2026 (17:00 hrs)
*   **Application Last Date:** 27th April 2026 (13:00 hrs)
*   **Exam Date:** 12th July 2026 (Sunday)

**Application Fees:**

*   APST Candidates: ₹150
*   Other Candidates: ₹200
*   PwBD Candidates: Exempted

**Vacancies by Subject:**

| Post / Subject        | Un-Reserved | Reserved for APST | Total APPSC TGT Vacancy 2026 |

| TGT (English)         | 8           | 28               | 36                            |

| TGT (Political Science)| 6           | 22               | 28                            |
| ... (See full notification for all subjects) ... |             |                |                               |

**Age Limit:** 21-35 years (as of 27th April 2026). Relaxable for APST, PwBD, and other categories as per government rules.

**Apply Online:** Visit [https://appsc.gov.in](https://appsc.gov.in)

website_content.title: CBSE Class 10 Result 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## CBSE Class 10 Result 2026

The CBSE Class 10 Result 2026 is expected to be declared in mid-April 2026. Evaluation of the February-March 2026 exams is complete or nearing completion.

**Exam Period:** February 17 to March 11, 2026
**Number of Students:** Approximately 25 lakh

**How to Check Online:**

1.  Visit [results.cbse.nic.in](results.cbse.nic.in) or [cbse.gov.in](cbse.gov.in).
2.  Click on the “CBSE Class 10 Result 2026” link.
3.  Enter your Roll Number, School Number, Admit Card ID, and Date of Birth.
4.  Click Submit.

**Alternative Methods:**

*   DigiLocker (preferred)
*   UMANG App
*   SMS service
*   IVRS

**DigiLocker Access:**

1.  Visit [digilocker.gov.in](digilocker.gov.in).
2.  Sign in using Aadhaar or registered credentials.
3.  Go to Issued Documents → Education section.
4.  Select CBSE.
5.  Choose Class 10 Marksheet 2026.
6.  Enter Roll Number and other details.
7.  Download the PDF.

website_content.title: Karnataka SSLC English Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Karnataka SSLC English Second Language Answer Key 2026

The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB) conducted the SSLC English Second Language (SL) exam on **March 25, 2026**, from **10:00 AM to 1:15 PM**. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Conducting Board:** Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board (KSEAB)
*   **Exam Name:** Karnataka SSLC English Second Language 2026
*   **Exam Date:** March 25, 2026
*   **Exam Time:** 10:00 AM – 1:15 PM
*   **Total Marks:** 100 (80 Theory + 20 Internal Assessment)

**Unofficial Answer Key:**

Subject experts have prepared an unofficial answer key shortly after the exam. Students can use this to estimate their score. The theory paper is worth 80 marks.

**How to Calculate Your Score:**

1.  Download the unofficial answer key.
2.  Match your answers.
3.  Add marks for correct answers.
4.  No negative marking (generally).
5.  Calculate total score out of 80.

**How to Download:**

website_content.title: AP SSC Physics Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## AP SSC Physics Answer Key 2026

The Board of Secondary Education, Andhra Pradesh (BSEAP) conducted the SSC Physical Science (Physics & Chemistry) exam on March 25, 2026, from 9:30 AM to 11:30 AM (with some sources indicating an extended window till 12:15 PM). This answer key is unofficial, prepared by subject experts to help students estimate their scores.

**Exam Highlights:**

| Particulars | Details |
|---|---| 
| Conducting Board | Board of Secondary Education, Andhra Pradesh (BSEAP) |
| Exam Name | AP SSC Physical Science (Physics) 2026 |
| Exam Date | March 25, 2026 |
| Exam Time | 9:30 AM – 11:30 AM |
| Total Marks (Physics Paper) | 50 Marks |
| Total Science Marks | 100 (50 Physical Science + 50 Biological Science) |

**How to Calculate Your Score:**

1. Download the unofficial answer key.
2. Match your answers with the correct solutions.
3. Add marks for correct answers.
4. No negative marking (generally).
5. Calculate your total score out of 50.

**Sample Questions & Answers:**

*   **Question:** Why is the series arrangement not used for domestic circuits?
    **Answer:** In a series circuit, if one component fails, the entire circuit breaks. Appliances don't receive the required voltage.
*   **Question:** Define dispersion of light.
    **Answer:** Dispersion is the splitting of white light into its constituent seven colours (VIBGYOR) through a transparent medium.
*   **Question:** Name one metal which will not displace hydrogen from dilute acids.
    **Answer:** Copper (Cu).

**How to Download:**

*   **Unofficial Answer Key:** Available from subject experts and coaching institutes.
*   **Topics Covered:** Python Programming, DBMS, Computer Networks, Case Study/Application-Based Questions.

*   DBMS: Easy to Moderate

website_content.title: ISRO URSC Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## ISRO URSC Scientist/Engineer & Medical Officer Recruitment 2026

**Organization:** U R Rao Satellite Centre (URSC), Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)

**Posts:** Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’, Scientist/Engineer ‘SD’, Medical Officer ‘SC’

**Total Vacancies:** 4

**Job Location:** URSC, Bengaluru and other ISRO/DOS Centres/Units across India

**Application Dates:**
*   **Start Date:** 23 March 2026
*   **Last Date:** 12 April 2026

**Age Limit (as of 12.04.2026):**
*   Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’: Up to 28 years
*   Scientist/Engineer ‘SD’: Up to 35 years
*   Medical Officer ‘SC’: Up to 35 years

**Pay Scale:**
*   Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’: Level 10 (Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500)
*   Scientist/Engineer ‘SD’: Level 11 (Rs. 67,100 – 2,08,700)
*   Medical Officer ‘SC’: Level 10 (Rs. 56,100 – 1,77,500) + NPA @ 20%

**Essential Qualifications:**
*   **SC:** B.E/B.Tech (65% or CGPA 6.84/10) in relevant engineering discipline.
*   **SD:** PhD in Thin Films / Material Science / Applied Optics / Metallurgy with mandated M.Sc./M.Tech and B.E/B.Tech/B.Sc.
*   **Medical Officer SC:** MBBS with 2 years’ experience and NMC/State Medical Council registration.

**Application Mode:** Online only

**Official Websites:** www.isro.gov.in and www.ursc.gov.in

**Application Fee:** Rs. 750/- for Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ & Medical Officer ‘SC’ (Refundable for eligible candidates). No fee for Scientist/Engineer ‘SD’.

**Exam Highlights:**

| Particulars          | Details                               |
|----------------------|---------------------------------------|
| Conducting Board     | Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE), Tamil Nadu |
| Exam Name            | Tamil Nadu SSLC Maths 2026            |
| Exam Date            | March 25, 2026                         |
| Exam Time            | 10:00 AM – 1:15 PM                    |
| Reading Time          | 10:00 AM – 10:10 AM                    |
| Verification Time     | 10:10 AM – 10:15 AM                    |
| Writing Time          | 10:15 AM – 1:15 PM                    |
| Total Marks          | 100 Marks                              |

**Unofficial Answer Key:**

Since DGE Tamil Nadu does not release an official answer key immediately, coaching institutes and subject experts have published unofficial answer keys. These keys can be used for quick performance evaluation.

*   [Tamil Nadu SSLC Maths Unofficial Question and Answer 2026](Click Here)

**How to Calculate Your Score:**

1.  Download the unofficial answer key.
2.  Match your answers.
3.  Add marks for correct answers.
4.  No negative marking.
5.  Calculate total score (out of 100).

website_content.title: UKMSSB Pharmacist Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## UKMSSB Pharmacist Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB) has released the provisional answer key for the Pharmacist (Allopathic) examination held on **22 March 2026**. 

**Key Details:**

*   **Organization:** Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB)
*   **Post Name:** Pharmacist (Allopathic)
*   **Total Vacancies:** 62
*   **Exam Date:** 22 March 2026 (11:00 AM - 01:00 PM)
*   **Answer Key Released:** 24 March 2026
*   **Objection Deadline:** 30 March 2026 (11:59:59 PM)
*   **Objection Fee:** ₹50 per question

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit [ukmssb.org](ukmssb.org)
2.  Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section.
3.  Click on "UKMSSB Pharmacist Answer Key 2026".
4.  Login with Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth.

**How to Raise Objection:**

1.  Login to your candidate portal on [ukmssb.org](ukmssb.org).
2.  Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section.
3.  Select the question and submit with supporting documents (if any).
4.  Pay the objection fee.

**Important Dates:**

*   Exam Conducted: 22 March 2026
*   Answer Key Released: 24 March 2026
*   Objection Deadline: 30 March 2026
*   Final Answer Key (Expected): After 30 March 2026

**Marking Scheme:**

*   Correct Answer: +1 mark
*   Wrong Answer: -0.25 mark
*   Unattempted: 0 marks

website_content.title: Pourakarmika Recruitment 2026 - Tumakuru
website_content.markdown_content:
## Tumakuru Pourakarmika Recruitment 2026

The District Urban Development Cell, Tumakuru, is recruiting 238 Pourakarmika posts across Tumakuru City Corporation, Tiptur City Municipal Council, and Sira City Municipal Council.

**Key Details:**

*   **Post Name:** Pourakarmika
*   **Total Vacancies:** 238
*   **ULBs Covered:** Tumakuru City Corporation (175), Tiptur City Municipal Council (24), Sira City Municipal Council (39)
*   **Application Mode:** Offline
*   **Age Limit:** 18-50 years as of the notification date.
*   **Essential Experience:** Minimum 1 year of service as outsourced/daily wage/contract Pourakarmika/Loader/UGD Helper/Cleaner/Helper/Waste processing or sanitation worker with ESI and PF.
*   **Eligibility:** Existing civic sanitation workers, family members of deceased Pourakarmikas, and identified manual scavenger victims.
*   **Important Dates:**
    *   Notification Date: 23-03-2026
    *   Application Form Availability: 25-03-2026
    *   Last Date to Apply: 08-04-2026 (5:00 PM)

**Application Process:**

1.  Obtain the application form from the respective ULB offices.
2.  Fill the form carefully.
3.  Submit the filled application along with required documents in person to the concerned ULB office by 08-04-2026.

**Official Website:** www.tumakurududc.mrc.gov.in

website_content.title: RBI Grade B Officer Final Result 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RBI Grade B Officer Final Result 2026 OUT! 

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released the final result for Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) - General posts on **24-03-2026**. Candidates who appeared in the Phase-I, Phase-II, and Interview rounds can now check their final selection status.

**Key Details:**

*   **Recruiting Body:** Reserve Bank of India Services Board
*   **Post Name:** Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) - General
*   **Result Type:** Final Result (After Interview)
*   **Result Declaration Date:** 24-03-2026
*   **Exam Stages:** Phase-I → Phase-II → Interview → Final Selection

**How to Check:**

1.  Visit the official website: opportunities.rbi.org.in
2.  Go to the "Results" or "Opportunities@RBI" section
3.  Click on the link for "Final Result - Recruitment of Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) - General - PY 2025"
4.  Download the PDF file (RNRC24032026.pdf)
5.  Search your Roll Number in the list of selected candidates

**Important Instructions:**

*   Download and save the final result PDF immediately.
*   Take printouts for future reference.
*   Wait for official joining instructions and offer letter from RBI.
*   Keep all original documents ready for verification.
*   Verify your details and report any discrepancies to RBI immediately.

Note: One candidate's result is kept in abeyance due to an ongoing court case.

website_content.markdown_content:
**UPPSC Assistant Teacher Admit Card 2026 OUT!**

Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) has released the admit card for the Assistant Teacher recruitment exam on **26 March 2026**. Candidates who applied can download their hall ticket from the official website.

**Key Details:**

*   **Release Date:** 26 March 2026
*   **Post Name:** Assistant Teacher
*   **Official Website:** uppsc.up.nic.in

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website: uppsc.up.nic.in
2.  Navigate to the "Admit Card" or "Latest Announcements" section.
3.  Click on "UPPSC Assistant Teacher Admit Card 2026".
4.  Enter your Application/Registration Number, Date of Birth, and Captcha Code.
5.  Download and save the PDF.
6.  Take 2-3 printouts.

**Important Documents to Carry:**

*   Admit Card (printed copy)
*   Valid Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, Driving License, PAN Card, Passport)
*   Recent passport-size photographs (2 copies)
*   Blue/Black ballpoint pen

**Pro Tip:** Check all details on your admit card immediately after download and report any discrepancies to the UPPSC helpdesk within 24 hours.

website_content.title: India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## India Post GDS 2nd Merit List 2026 - Expected Soon!

The Department of Posts is expected to release the 2nd Merit List for Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS), Branch Postmaster (BPM), and Assistant Branch Postmaster (ABPM) posts in the **4th week of March 2026 or 1st week of April 2026**. 

The Document Verification (DV) process for the 1st Merit List concluded on March 23, 2026. The 1st list was released on March 6, 2026.

**Key Details:**

*   **Recruiting Body:** India Post (Department of Posts)
*   **Post Name:** Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS), BPM, ABPM
*   **Merit List Type:** 2nd Merit List (Supplementary)
*   **Expected Release Date:** 4th Week of March 2026 or 1st Week of April 2026
*   **1st Merit List Released On:** March 6, 2026
*   **DV Process for 1st List Ended:** March 23, 2026
*   **Total Vacancies:** 28,636 (approx.)
*   **Selection Stages:** Merit (10th Marks) → Document Verification → Medical Test → Appointment

**How to Check:**

1.  Visit the official website: [https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in](https://indiapostgdsonline.gov.in) or [https://indiapost.gov.in](https://indiapost.gov.in)
2.  Click on the “Shortlisted Candidates” or “List of Shortlisted Candidates” section.
3.  Select your Postal Circle / State.
4.  Download the PDF file for the “2nd Merit List 2026” or Supplementary List.
5.  Search for your Registration Number or Name using Ctrl + F.

**Important Instructions:**

*   Check the official website daily after March 25, 2026.
*   Download and save the merit list PDF immediately.
*   Keep original documents ready for Document Verification.

*   **CPT Date:** 04 April 2026 at 10:00 AM

*   Carry original documents and ID proof.

For detailed information and to check your status, visit the official website.

website_content.title: Purnea University Admit Card 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Purnea University Admit Card 2026 - Download Now!

Purnea University has released the admit card for Undergraduate (UG) semester and end-semester examinations 2026. Students can download their hall tickets from the official portal using their registration or roll number.

**Key Highlights:**

*   **University:** Purnea University, Bihar
*   **Exam Type:** UG Semester / End-Semester Examinations 2026
*   **Courses:** BA, BSc, BCom and other Undergraduate programmes
*   **Admit Card Mode:** Online download only
*   **Credentials Required:** Registration number / roll number / user ID and password

**Important Dates:**

*   **Admit Card Release Date:** 26 March 2026
*   **UG 1st Semester Exam Start Date:** 30 March 2026
*   **UG 1st Semester Exam Period:** 30 March 2026 to 15 April 2026

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website: [https://purneau.ac.in](https://purneau.ac.in)
2.  Navigate to the “Examination” or “Student Corner” section.
3.  Click on the “Admit Card” or “Download Admit Card” link.
4.  Enter your registration number, roll number, or user ID and password.
5.  Download and print the admit card in PDF format.

**Details on Admit Card:**

*   Student’s name, photograph, and signature
*   Father’s and mother’s name
*   Registration number, roll number, and course name
*   Exam centre details
*   Reporting time and exam duration

**Important Instructions:**

*   Carry a printed copy of the admit card and a valid photo ID.
*   Do not carry mobile phones or any electronic devices.
*   Reach the exam centre at least 30 minutes before reporting time.

website_content.title: RRB Group D Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## RRB Group D Answer Key 2026 – Overview

The **RRB Group D Answer Key 2026** is now available for candidates who appeared in the CBT from **27 November 2025 to 10 February 2026**. Candidates can check their responses and estimate scores.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** RRB Group D CBT 2026 (CEN 08/2024 Level 1 posts)
*   **Post Name:** Group D / Level 1 posts in Indian Railways
*   **Answer Key Status:** Provisional - Objection window open
*   **Release Date:** 17 February 2026
*   **Objection Window:** Open until **23 February 2026 (11:59 p.m.)**
*   **Objection Fee:** ₹50 per question (refundable if valid)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit rrb.digialm.com or your regional RRB website (e.g., rrbcdg.gov.in).
2.  Login using your User ID/Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth.
3.  Download the answer key, response sheet, and question paper as a PDF.

**How to Raise an Objection:**

1.  Login to rrb.digialm.com.
2.  Click on “Objection Tracker / Challenge Answer Key”.
3.  Select the Question ID and specify the correct option.
4.  Upload supporting evidence.
5.  Pay the objection fee.

website_content.title: GATE COAP 2026 Registration Open
website_content.markdown_content:
## GATE COAP 2026 Registration - Important Details

Registration for GATE COAP 2026 is now open! This is the centralized platform for GATE-qualified candidates to accept or reject M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des offers from participating IITs.

**Key Highlights:**

*   **Portal Name:** GATE Common Offer Acceptance Portal (COAP) 2026
*   **Purpose:** View and accept IIT M.Tech/M.Plan/M.Des offers based on GATE scores.
*   **Conducting Institute:** IIT Roorkee
*   **Academic Year:** 2026-27
*   **Registration Fee:** No separate fee.
*   **Eligible GATE Years:** GATE 2024, GATE 2025, GATE 2026
*   **Who Can Register:** Indian citizens with a valid GATE score.

**Important Dates:**

*   Registration Begins: Mid-March 2026 (Active)
*   Round 1 (View Offers): May 11, 2026 (10 AM) - May 13, 2026 (9 AM)
*   Round 2 (View Offers): May 17, 2026 (10 AM) - May 20, 2026 (9 AM)
*   Round 3 (View Offers): May 24, 2026 (10 AM) - May 27, 2026 (9 AM)
*   Round 4 (View Offers): May 31, 2026 (10 AM) - June 3, 2026 (9 AM)
*   Final Round (Round 10): June 8, 2026 (10 AM) - July 10, 2026 (9 AM)

**Registration Steps:**

1.  Visit the COAP 2026 portal.
2.  Click on “Register” / “New User Registration”.
3.  Enter GATE details (Registration ID, paper code, year, score).
4.  Fill personal information.
5.  Create login credentials.
6.  Submit and verify.

**Remember:** COAP registration is compulsory, but it doesn't replace individual IIT applications.

website_content.title: MAH MCA CET 2026 Admit Card Released
website_content.markdown_content:
## MAH MCA CET 2026 Admit Card – Key Details

The admit card for the MAH MCA CET 2026 has been released and is available for download on the State CET Cell, Maharashtra’s official website.

**Key Details:**

*   **Exam Name:** MAH MCA CET 2026
*   **Exam Date:** 30 March 2026
*   **Admit Card Release:** Fourth week of March 2026 (around 25 March 2026)
*   **Mode of Exam:** Computer-Based Test (CBT)

**How to Download:**

1.  Visit the official website.
2.  Navigate to the “CETs” or “Admit Card / Hall Ticket” section.
3.  Select the examination year 2026.
4.  Enter your registered email ID / application number and password.
5.  Download and print the admit card (2-3 copies).

**Documents Required for Exam Day:**

*   Printed Admit Card (with photograph)
*   Original Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar, PAN, Voter ID, Driving License, Bank Passbook, or College/University ID)

**Important Note:** Verify all details on the admit card and contact the CET Cell for any discrepancies.

*   **Practical Exams:** March 17 - April 1, 2026
*   **Theory Exams:** April 4 - May 8, 2026

**Exam Timing:** 2:30 PM to 5:30 PM daily.

1.  Visit [nios.ac.in](https://nios.ac.in).
2.  Navigate to "Notifications" or "Examinations & Results".
3.  Select "Public Examinations Date Sheet 2026".

**Practical Exam Schedule Highlights:**

**Class 10:**
*   March 17 – March 20, 2026: Science & Technology, Home Science, Carnatic Sangeet, Folk Art
*   March 21 – March 24, 2026: Painting, Maths, Hindustani Music, Data Entry Operations, Natyakala
*   March 25 – March 28, 2026: Hair Care & Styling, Hand & Foot Care, Bakery & Confectionery, Basic Computing, DTP, Yoga, Indian Sign Language
*   March 29 – April 1, 2026: Cutting & Tailoring, Dress Making, Beauty Culture, Indian Embroidery, Beauty Therapy

**Class 12:**
*   March 17-20, 2026: Home Science, Biology, Geography, Painting
*   Later March: Computer Science, Mass Communication

*   **Student Portal:** sdmis.nios.ac.in

*   **Exam Name:** State Service Examination (SSE) Prelims 2026
*   **Result Date:** March 25, 2026
*   **Exam Date:** February 22, 2026
*   **Selection Stages:** Prelims → Mains (May 16-19, 2026) → Interview
*   **Official Website:** [psc.cg.gov.in](psc.cg.gov.in)

1.  Visit [psc.cg.gov.in](psc.cg.gov.in)

**Important Links:**

*   **Merit List PDF:** Available
*   **Mains Exam Schedule:** May 16-19, 2026

website_content.title: Jharkhand Madhyamik Acharya Answer Key 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## Jharkhand Madhyamik Acharya Answer Key 2026 Released!

The Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC) has released the provisional answer key for the Madhyamik Acharya Combined Competitive Examination (JTMACCE-2025). 

**Exam Dates:** 15 January 2026 – 22 February 2026
**Total Vacancies:** 1373
**Answer Key Released:** 23 March 2026
**Objection Deadline:** 28 March 2026

**How to Download:**
1. Visit [jssc.jharkhand.gov.in](jssc.jharkhand.gov.in).
2. Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Important Links" section.
3. Click on "JSSC Madhyamik Acharya Answer Key 2026".
4. Login with your credentials.
5. Download the PDF.

**How to Raise Objections:**
1. Login to the candidate portal.
2. Go to the "Objection / Challenge" section.
3. Select the question(s) to challenge.

website_content.title: Basara IIIT Admission 2026-27
website_content.markdown_content:
## Basara IIIT Admission 2026-27 - Overview

Rajiv Gandhi University of Knowledge Technologies (RGUKT), Basar, has announced admissions for the 6-Year Integrated B.Tech programme for the academic year 2026-27. This is for students who have passed SSC (10th class) in the current year.

**Key Highlights:**

*   **Admission Name:** Basara IIIT 6-Year Integrated B.Tech Admission
*   **University:** Rajiv Gandhi University of Knowledge Technologies (RGUKT), Basar
*   **Course:** 6-Year Integrated B.Tech Programme
*   **Academic Year:** 2026-27
*   **Mode of Application:** Online
*   **Eligibility:** SSC (10th class) pass students (current year)
*   **Selection Basis:** SSC marks/GPA and RGUKT rules

**Important Dates (Tentative):**

*   Notification Release: March 15, 2026
*   Online Application Start: March 18, 2026
*   Online Application Last Date: May 10, 2026
*   Special Category Deadline: May 20, 2026
*   Provisional Selection List: May 30, 2026
*   Phase I Counseling: June 5, 2026

**Eligibility Criteria:**

*   Passed SSC (10th class) or equivalent from a recognized board.
*   Required GPA/marks as per RGUKT Basar admission rules.
*   Generally, schooling completed in Telangana.
*   Age Limit: Usually around 18 years as of June 1, 2026 (relaxations for reserved categories).

**Programme Details:**

*   6-Year Integrated B.Tech (2-year PU phase + 4-year B.Tech phase).
*   Fully residential programme.

**Application Fee:**

*   Varies by category (General/OC/BC higher, SC/ST/PH concessional).
*   Payment through online gateways (credit/debit card, net banking, TS Online/MeeSeva).

**How to Apply:**

1.  Visit the official RGUKT Basar admission website.
2.  Complete registration with SSC details.
3.  Pay the application fee.
4.  Fill the detailed application form.
5.  Upload scanned documents (SSC marks memo, caste certificate, etc.).
6.  Submit the application and print the form/receipt.

**Documents Required:**

*   SSC marks memo
*   Caste certificate (if applicable)
*   Income/EWS certificate (if applicable)
*   Residence/local status certificate
*   PH/CAP/NCC/Sports certificates (if applicable)
*   Photograph and signature

**Selection Process:**

*   Based on SSC GPA/marks and RGUKT rules.
*   Tie-breaking rules may apply.

**Important Note:** Always cross-check exact dates, fees, and seat details with the official notification.

website_content.title: MP FSL Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## MP Forensic Science Laboratory Recruitment 2026

The Madhya Pradesh Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) has announced a recruitment drive to fill **126 vacancies** for **Laboratory Technician, Laboratory Assistant, and Laboratory Attendant** positions.

**Key Details:**

*   **Posts:** Laboratory Technician (35), Laboratory Assistant (33), Laboratory Attendant (58)
*   **Eligibility:**
    *   Lab Technician: B.Sc/Forensic Science/Computer Science/Computer Application
    *   Lab Assistant: 12th (Science)
    *   Lab Attendant: 10th/12th (Science)
*   **Application Fee:** No fee
*   **Application Mode:** Online via MP Online portal
*   **Application Start Date:** 18 Mar 2026
*   **Application End Date:** 06 Apr 2026
*   **Age Limit:** 18-40 years (general male), with relaxation for women and reserved categories (up to 45-50 years).
*   **Pay Scale:**
    *   Lab Technician: Rs. 28,700–91,300
    *   Lab Assistant: Rs. 19,500–62,000
    *   Lab Attendant: Rs. 18,000–56,900

**Selection Process:**

1.  Shortlisting (5x rule based on qualifying exam marks)
2.  2-hour Written Exam (80% General Science, 20% General Knowledge)

**Important Links:**

*   [Official Notification PDF](https://chayan.mponline.gov.in/)
*   [Online Application Portal](https://chayan.mponline.gov.in/)

website_content.title: NSRY NAY Apprentice Recruitment 2026
website_content.markdown_content:
## NSRY NAY Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Indian Navy

The Naval Ship Repair Yard (NSRY) Kochi & Naval Aircraft Yard (NAY) Kochi are seeking applications for **240 Designated Trade Apprentice** positions under the Apprenticeship Act 1961.

**Key Details:**

*   **Total Vacancies:** 240
*   **Trades:** COPA, Electrician, Electronics Mechanic, Fitter, Machinist, Welder, Instrument Mechanic, Sheet Metal Worker, Draughtsman, and more (21 trades total).
*   **Eligibility:** Matric/10th (min 50% marks) & ITI Certificate (aggregate 65% marks) in relevant trade.
*   **Age Limit:** Above 18 years as of June 1, 2026 (No upper age limit).
*   **Application Mode:** Offline via India Post only.
*   **Application Fee:** None
*   **Last Date to Apply:** April 3, 2026
*   **Tentative Training Start Date:** July 2026

**Important Dates:**

*   Advertisement Published: March 28 – April 3, 2026
*   Application Submission Deadline: April 3, 2026

**Selection Process:** Shortlisting based on SSC & ITI marks, followed by Interview & Medical Test.

**Stipend:** As per Apprenticeship Act 1961 and MSDE norms.

**Note:** Employment after training is not obligatory.

**Key Highlights:**
